spacing_and_layout.general: Generous spacing philosophy (p-6 to p-12) for dashboard, but highly dense (p-2 to p-4) for POS order-taking screens to maximize efficiency.
spacing_and_layout.border_radius: max 8px (rounded-md) for technical precision, avoiding soft childish aesthetics.
spacing_and_layout.pos_grid: MODE B: Control Room Grid. 3-column layout (Categories 1-col, Items 2-col, Cart/KOT 1-col) spanning 100% height.
spacing_and_layout.surfaces: Grid Borders for technical dashboards. Flat backgrounds with 1px subtle borders (#E6E4DE). Avoid large drop shadows; use sharp inner contrasts.

motion_and_interactions.hover_states: Slight lift (-translate-y-0.5) with border color darkening.
motion_and_interactions.pos_interactions: Micro-interactions on item click (scale-95 to simulate physical tap).
motion_and_interactions.sync_indicator: Pulse animation for offline/online sync status indicator.

components.buttons: Flat, sharp edges (rounded-md). High contrast text. Use primary color for critical actions (Pay, Send to Kitchen), subtle outlines for secondary actions.
components.cards: Cardless approach preferred. Use pure HTML + Tailwind with border-collapse and subtle borders for data grids. If cards are needed, use flat white background with 1px border.
components.tables: Dense, crisp lines. Hover state on rows. Use Shadcn Table.
components.forms: Shadcn inputs. Solid background (white) inside gray app background. Focus ring using primary color (#E06C4C).
components.navigation: Sidebar for core modules (Dashboard, POS, Kitchen, Tables, Settings). Sticky top header for contextual actions, user profile, and sync indicator.

icons.style: Minimal, functional, scaling with text (w-4 h-4 for body text, w-5 h-5 for headers).

login_background.description: Blurred restaurant interior with people for a warm, inviting login screen.

instructions_to_main_agent: Configure Tailwind with the custom color palette, focusing on #E06C4C as the primary action color and #FAF9F6 as the app background.
Ensure the layout is highly dense on the POS order-taking screen. Use a 100vh layout with a strict grid (Categories | Items | KOT Cart) avoiding unnecessary scrolling.

Integrate the Swiggy (#FC8019) and Zomato (#CB202D) colors distinctly in the incoming online orders panel, avoiding full-screen brand colors, instead using colored badges, thin top borders, or subtle background tints.
Implement the 'Hybrid Offline/Online' sync indicator in the global header (e.g., a green pulsing dot for online, gray for offline, with a sync queue counter).
For the Kitchen Order Tickets (KOT) screen, use a Kanban-style board (Received, Preparing, Ready) leveraging Grid Borders for clear segregation.
Table floor plan should be a visual grid showing status colors (Free: White, Occupied: Terracotta, Reserved: Forest Green).
Avoid generic soft shadows; use sharp 1px borders and distinct typographic hierarchy.
Ensure all interactive elements have 'data-testid' attributes (e.g., data-testid='pos-add-item-btn', data-testid='checkout-btn').
